input.prompt:
My .NET MAUI page shows blank labels even though my ViewModel properties
have values. Here is my XAML:
```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml">
    <VerticalStackLayout>
        <Label Text="{Binding Title}" />
        <Label Text="{Binding Description}" />
    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```
What am I doing wrong?

input.initialPrompt:
My .NET MAUI page shows blank labels even though my ViewModel properties
have values. Here is my XAML:
```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml">
    <VerticalStackLayout>
        <Label Text="{Binding Title}" />
        <Label Text="{Binding Description}" />
    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```
What am I doing wrong?

data.content:
You're missing the `BindingContext`. The bindings have no source object to pull values from.

**Fix — set the BindingContext** (pick one approach):

**In code-behind:**
```csharp
public MainPage()
{
    InitializeComponent();
    BindingContext = new MyViewModel();
}
```

**Or in XAML:**
```xml
<ContentPage xmlns:vm="clr-namespace:MyApp.ViewModels"
             ...>
    <ContentPage.BindingContext>
        <vm:MyViewModel />
    </ContentPage.BindingContext>
    ...
</ContentPage>
```

Also make sure your ViewModel implements `INotifyPropertyChanged` (or inherits `ObservableObject` from the MVVM Toolkit) so the UI updates when properties change.
data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about a common .NET MAUI data binding issue. The labels are blank despite the ViewModel having values. Looking at the XAML, the most likely issue is that the `BindingContext` is never set, so the bindings have nothing to resolve against.

There could also be a missing `INotifyPropertyChanged` implementation, but the most obvious issue from the XAML alone is no `BindingContext`.
